$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the internal bridge REQUEST envelope shape (BridgeReq). The runtime validator is the hand-written Zod schema in packages/shared/src/envelope.ts, whose z.toJSONSchema() output is diffed against this file in CI (the equivalence test) - so the TS types are inferred, not hand-synced. OpArgs is the union of every tool's inputSchema properties in tools.json minus the server-consumed `browser` routing argument; the same equivalence test enforces that. This describes the CURRENT wide envelope: `op` is a plain string and `args` is a flat bag of optional fields shared across all ops; the extension additionally narrows args per op against the generated per-op validators (packages/shared/src/ops.gen.ts).
title: BridgeReq
description: A request from the MCP server, forwarded through the native host to the extension. Shape: { id, op, tabId?, args }.

id.description: Correlation id echoed back on the matching BridgeResp. Monotonic integer in practice; string is accepted for forward-compatibility. Integers are bounded to the JS-safe range: the extension is a JS runtime, so anything larger has already lost precision in JSON.parse before validation.

op.description: The operation name (a tool's underlying op). Enumerated by contracts/tools.json; kept as a plain string here, with the per-op narrowing enforced by the generated validators.

tabId.description: Optional target tab. When omitted, the handler resolves the active tab.

browser.description: The label of the browser connection the MCP server routed this request to (from the tool call's `browser` argument, or the sole/default connection). Informational for the extension: each native-messaging port already belongs to exactly one browser.

OpArgs.description: Arguments an op may carry: the union of every tool's inputSchema properties in tools.json, minus `browser` (consumed by the MCP server for routing, never forwarded). Every field is optional here; the per-op required fields are enforced by the generated per-op validators on the extension side and by the host's payload builders. Integers are bounded to the JS-safe range (see `id`).
